name: Belinda Biafore
subject: Let's close the gap..
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www
body:
Fellow Democrat,

Today is April 10th 2018, Equal Pay Day. This day symbolizes the date women must work up until to make the same amount that men made working in 2017.

We have to do better. Women across the country make roughly $.80 for every $1 their male counterparts make. That's a 20% pay gap.

Equal pay for equal work. It's THAT SIMPLE.

Can we count on you today to join us in fighting to close the 20% pay gap by donating $20?

Rankings in the workplace and in political offices are rapidly becoming dominated by women, but it's not enough when we still face discrimination (of many kinds) in the workplace.

Women rise year after year, higher and higher, chipping away at that glass ceiling. How do we expect our future generations of women to break that glass ceiling if we aren't provided equal opportunity across the board? & that includes equal pay for equal work.

Can we count on you to join Democrats in supporting women in the workplace?

Sincerely,

Belinda Biafore, Chair

name: The White House
subject: ‘We will liberate our country’
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=e67aad937a6e99e2cbf9c8215109da2a19d772023e681dcedde4e2d44f6e699d83c09974da61e366970287655a046f30cf391e13f008f12f

‘We will liberate our country’

Less than a month ago, President Donald J. Trump laid out a bold vision for defeating the opioid epidemic. In the weeks since, his Administration has taken further action to combat the crisis on all fronts:

- On March 23, President Trump signed the omnibus funding bill, providing about $4 billion for addiction treatment and prevention, drug courts, and other tools to combat the crisis.
-
Last week, Attorney General Jeff Sessions announced the results of the first operation led by the new Joint Criminal Opioid Darknet Enforcement (J-CODE) Team, which resulted in 8 arrests and the seizure of drugs and weapons.
- The U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) conducted a 45-day surge in February and March to investigate medical professionals who were prescribing disproportionately high numbers of opioids. The surge led to 28 arrests and 147 revoked medical registrations.

“Failure is not an option. Addiction is not our future,” President Trump says. “We will liberate our country from this crisis.”

More:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=e67aad937a6e99e285f77e5e40fa234bdc500289329d7d8b171be93d22dd0b3be278bef3be1601b74f605c9c9cffac14afb38e1b38b4b085
How the Administration is working every day to bring an end to the opioid crisis

Step 1 to better infrastructure: Stop Washington infighting

Investing in a rebuild of America’s crumbling infrastructure is important, but President Trump knows funding will only help if we fix our country’s broken approval process. Inefficient environmental reviews have led to unnecessary delays, depriving our communities of needed projects.

Yesterday, President Trump gathered Federal agency leaders to sign the One Federal Decision Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), establishing a quicker process for environmental reviews of major infrastructure projects. Instead of expecting project sponsors to navigate multiple decision-making layers, one lead agency will be responsible for navigating each project through the entire process.

Previous interagency disputes sometimes remained unresolved for years. Americans deserve better than this Byzantine system. With yesterday’s MOU, President Trump is helping repair it.

Learn more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=e67aad937a6e99e2ecf48a27985f04d6a396737b206ecabd695e50c40be32be46065218d71523f9b5c20d53167cdfec7f2f1f134cfa4b50c
Improving reviews, streamlining decisions, and cutting costly delays

President meets with military leaders

Top U.S. defense and military leaders met with President Trump last night, joining the Commander in Chief for dinner and meeting beforehand to review progress in protecting America’s interests at home and abroad.

The President reiterated his commitment to rebuilding our Nation’s Armed Forces, including through the budget agreement he made with Congress that invests more than $1.4 trillion in defense over the next two years. This budget supports efforts to recover readiness, grow the force, buy more modern equipment, and give our troops a much deserved pay raise.

Yesterday, President Trump also addressed this weekend’s chemical weapons attack in Syria. “This is about humanity, we're talking about humanity, and it can't be allowed to happen," the President said. He reiterated that all options remain on the table for a U.S. response.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=e67aad937a6e99e2539bd5de37f43b46844f06d485fe0a456404b6655c37c397f5e3802563a6ab5a7a529d1d24fbf0727a06caf8222130fd
Read the White House’s full statement about President Trump’s meeting with military leaders.

Photo of the Day

First Lady Melania Trump participates in a listening session with students | April 9, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump and Vice President Mike Pence will meet and participate in a working lunch with the Amir of the State of Qatar.

In the afternoon, the President will host the 2017 NCAA Football National Champions: The Alabama Crimson Tide at the White House.

| | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/gqenu-6JsLXKHlHXBPl8JhJfLHHQMTX_-XXjpHkkZd4=/kCNYdtQ9pi6d7oTs5PB-nneXHwaLwZkbg1eRNnijAvs=?am=10&recipientId=GYKgujd1qXAheZskgLys31&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&)

Friends: **Pennsylvania does abysmally on the representation of women in government**. Believe it or not, we do not have a single woman in our delegation to the House of Representatives. [**Nina Ahmad is running for Lieutenant Governor and is worthy of your support: Your vote, your time and your financial resources if possible. She is a strong feminist and a bold progressive.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/gqenu-6JsLXKHlHXBPl8JhJfLHHQMTX_-XXjpHkkZd4=/QLAC-bLGpKJNLHbcME8I-fNsJi4iHv57Bn_OlBdkiP4=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/gqenu-6JsLXKHlHXBPl8JhJfLHHQMTX_-XXjpHkkZd4=/QLAC-bLGpKJNLHbcME8I-fNsJi4iHv57Bn_OlBdkiP4=) Pat West and I have known Nina Ahmad for several years and have been asking her to run for congress for some time. She daringly threw her hat into the ring to run against Bob Brady in PA-01 in December. Once the courts effectively did away with that district after redrawing congressional lines, Nina didn't quit. In fact, she decided to bring her campaign of equality and justice to all of Pennsylvania in the Lieutenant Governor's race. That's the type of fighter she is. Nina is a first generation immigrant from Bangladesh. She is incredibly accomplished: she received a Ph.D. from the University of Pennsylvania in Chemistry, worked with her husband on community economic development, ran the local chapter of NOW, has been very involved in local politics, and most recently was Deputy Mayor for Public Engagement in the Kenney administration. She was a member of Obama's advisory group on Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders. She is a star. She will bring a fresh outlook to Harrisburg. Her first priority is jobs, jobs, jobs. Her work in economic development makes her particularly qualified to address this issue. Further, being a woman, an immigrant, a person of color helps her understand the experiences of a broad swath of Pennsylvania voters. [](https://act.myngp.com/el/gqenu-6JsLXKHlHXBPl8JhJfLHHQMTX_-XXjpHkkZd4=/QLAC-bLGpKJNLHbcME8I-fNsJi4iHv57Bn_OlBdkiP4=) [_**There is a fundraiser for her on Wednesday April 11, 2018**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/gqenu-6JsLXKHlHXBPl8JhJfLHHQMTX_-XXjpHkkZd4=/K8XLUuh5c9Hy5mlT9z5cBfcvdIxR-Dyp2Adw4wyJW7o=). It is at a fabulous museum of found art, which I have been fortunate to visit. **Here is how you can help:**
* **[Click the invitation below](https://act.myngp.com/el/gqenu-6JsLXKHlHXBPl8JhJfLHHQMTX_-XXjpHkkZd4=/K8XLUuh5c9Hy5mlT9z5cBfcvdIxR-Dyp2Adw4wyJW7o=) to purchase a ticket;**
* **Come to the event if you 're in town;**
* **Forward this email to friends who may be interested in her campaign; and**
* **Sign up to volunteer through Nina 's website.**

Thank you. Wendy Wolf P.S. If you cannot attend this event but would still like to support this campaign, **[CLICK HERE.](https://act.myngp.com/el/gqenu-6JsLXKHlHXBPl8JhJfLHHQMTX_-XXjpHkkZd4=/QLAC-bLGpKJNLHbcME8I-fNsJi4iHv57Bn_OlBdkiP4=) ** [](https://act.myngp.com/el/gqenu-6JsLXKHlHXBPl8JhJfLHHQMTX_-XXjpHkkZd4=/hBTofJwT96RC7XkqfbpIpOuDYtc8uxVK57mcExXSbEo=) **[< <Click here for tickets>>](https://act.myngp.com/el/gqenu-6JsLXKHlHXBPl8JhJfLHHQMTX_-XXjpHkkZd4=/hBTofJwT96RC7XkqfbpIpOuDYtc8uxVK57mcExXSbEo=)** **Bethany Mission Art Gallery** 1527 Brandywine Street, Philadelphia PA 19130 Wednesday, April 11th, 2018 6:30 - 8:30PM

She was a member of Obama's advisory group on Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders. She is a star. She will bring a fresh outlook to Harrisburg. Her first priority is jobs, jobs, jobs. Her work in economic development makes her particularly qualified to address this issue. Further, being a woman, an immigrant, a person of color helps her understand the experiences of a broad swath of Pennsylvania voters. = =. It is at a fabulous museum of found art, which I have been fortunate to visit. Here is how you can help: = to purchase a ticket; Come to the event if you 're in town; Forward this email to friends who may be interested in her campaign; and Sign up to volunteer through Nina 's website. name: Joe Manchin
subject: Fair Pay
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Manchin for West Virginia
body:
The first vote I took in the United States Senate was for paycheck fairness, and I will continue to fight for it because people should earn the same pay for the same work. Period. |
---
[](https://secure.joemanchinwv.com/page/m/49819e3a/2efbf85c/623119b8/4c924c66/918813446/VEsH/ "http://www.joemanchinwv.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=manchin&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=JM_EM_PT_WV180DO_180410&source=JM_EM_PT_WV180DO_180410&refcode=JM_EM_PT_WV180DO_180410")

| Friend,

Today, Equal Pay Day, represents how far into the year that women must work to earn what men earned in the previous year.

The first vote I took in the United States Senate was for paycheck fairness, and I will continue to fight for it because people should earn the same pay for the same work. Period.

The fact that working women in West Virginia are earning 72 cents to every dollar a man makes just defies common sense. It shouldn't matter whether you're a woman or a man - you should be treated fairly and equally no matter what.

[**_Sign my petition today to demand that Mitch McConnell prioritize a vote for paycheck fairness._**](https://secure.joemanchinwv. name: Jay Inslee
subject: Rolling back emission standards
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
The EPA is caving in to corporate polluters. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79f7b/2f5ea88d/724818af/2cbcba4e/31771659/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/emission-standards?utm_source=ema_2018-04-10_emission-standards&source=ema_2018-04-10_emission-standards&utm_campaign=emission-standards&utm_medium=ema") Friend, Bad news. Big oil and corporate polluters just scored a huge victory. Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Scott Pruitt is revoking Obama-era standards that require new cars and light trucks to average more than 50 miles per gallon by 2025. These standards would have cut carbon dioxide emissions by an estimated six billion tons, while saving billions of barrels of oil. Those aren't just numbers -- these standards are critical for keeping our communities clean and healthy, and they would have been a major step forward for climate action. Pruitt's announcement shows he's putting corporate interests ahead of us -- and politics ahead of science. President Trump's erratic demands to roll back commonsense standards is purely short-sighted. [**Sign on to oppose the rollback of emission standards. Urge Congress to reject any attempt by the Trump administration to eliminate clean vehicle requirements. Sign on here.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79f7b/2f5ea88d/724818af/2cbcba4e/31771659/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/emission-standards?utm_source=ema_2018-04-10_emission-standards&source=ema_2018-04-10_emission-standards&utm_campaign=emission-standards&utm_medium=ema") This terrible decision is another example of political pandering by the Trump administration to powerful special interests and lobbyists. Instead of draining the swamp as he promised, the president is making it more toxic by putting corporate polluters ahead of working families and our health. We can't allow the Trump administration and the EPA to sell our children's future to well-heeled lobbyists and big donors. _It's a political decision that must be immediately reversed._ [**Sign on now. Tell President Trump and Scott Pruitt to keep blatant politics and big money out of environmental decision-making. Add your name here.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79f7b/2f5ea88d/724818af/2cbcba4e/31771659/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/emission-standards?utm_source=ema_2018-04-10_emission-standards&source=ema_2018-04-10_emission-standards&utm_campaign=emission-standards&utm_medium=ema") Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79f7b/2f5ea88d/724818af/2cbcba4e/31771659/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/emission-standards?utm_source=ema_2018-04-10_emission-standards&source=ema_2018-04-10_emission-standards&utm_campaign=emission-standards&utm_medium=ema")

name: Joe
subject: Washington is Broken
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
If there's two things people who get my emails know for sure, it's that Washington doesn't always work, and my campaign is always asking for money. And I know both of those things are frustrating -- s |
---

| | Peter,

If there's two things people who get my emails know for sure, it's that **Washington doesn 't always work**, and **my campaign is always asking for money**. And I know both of those things are frustrating -- so I want to explain.

**When I vote in the Senate, I vote the way that makes sense for Hoosiers -- and the Washington special interests just can 't stand that.** They're watching every move I make and every word I say -- even making up scorecards that rate how I line up with their agenda. All so they can try and replace my independent voice with a rubber stamp.

That's why Washington is broken... and why I have to keep asking for money, so that we have a shot at fighting back.

**Can you chip in today to help me keep standing up to Washington special interests?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
---
**[Express donate $10>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c205f/7377f838/31f3e6da/51fa83ef/658438347/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-washingtonisbroken?express_lane=true&refcode=20180410.1nd&amount=10")**
**[Express donate $25>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c205f/7377f838/31f3e6da/51fa83ee/658438347/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-washingtonisbroken?express_lane=true&refcode=20180410.1nd&amount=25")**
**[Express donate $50>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c205f/7377f838/31f3e6da/51fa83e9/658438347/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-washingtonisbroken?express_lane=true&refcode=20180410.1nd&amount=50")**
**[Express donate $100>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c205f/7377f838/31f3e6da/51fa83e8/658438347/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-washingtonisbroken?express_lane=true&refcode=20180410.1nd&amount=100")**
**[Or, donate a different amount>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c205f/7377f838/31f3e6da/51fa83eb/658438347/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-washingtonisbroken?refcode=20180410.1nd")**
**Those special interests are FURIOUS that I voted:**
* against massive tax cuts for corporations and the ultra rich
* to preserve health care access for Hoosiers
* against confirming Betsy DeVos as Secretary of Education

**Meanwhile, the same special interests are cozying up to Mitch McConnell and his crew** , filling up their campaign coffers in exchange for vote after vote for policies that line their pockets. They've already spent _millions_ in dark money to take out the folks who don't line up nicely with those scorecards (like me). That's disgraceful. Joe

| | **************** Friends,

Today is Equal Pay Day, the day that marks the additional days a woman must work in 2018 to earn as much as her male counterparts earned in 2017.

The gender pay gap is unacceptable. Every person deserves equal pay for equal work.

**[That's why we're sending an open letter to demand Congress work toward true pay equality, starting with passing the Paycheck Fairness Act, a bill co-sponsored by Kyrsten. We need 10,000 supporters like you to sign on to show Congress just how much support for pay equality there is. Add your name now >>](http://act.kyrstensinema. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ks-equalpay?amount=10&recurring=true&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Phoenix&state=AZ&zip=85002&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=sinema&utm_content=1+-+Thatrsquos+why+wersquore+sending+an+open&utm_campaign=KS_EM_FR_WL_180410_1_1&source=KS_EM_FR_WL_180410_1_1&refcode=KS_EM_FR_WL_180410_1_1")** | Dear Members of Congress,

On average, a woman in America makes only $.80 for every $1 that her male counterpart makes. This pay gap affects women of all careers and backgrounds, and disproportionately impacts women of color.

That means that the average woman must work until April 10, 2018, to make the same money as a man in her job would have made in 2017.

**This is unacceptable. We must continue striving for true pay equality, and that 's why I am standing with Kyrsten Sinema and calling on Congress to pass the Paycheck Fairness Act.**

For too long, women in the workplace have made significantly less than men. This hurts women and the families who rely on them.

**We must do everything in our power to make paychecks fair for everyone, and that starts with passing the Paycheck Fairness Act.**

**[Add my name!](http://act.kyrstensinema. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ks-equalpay?amount=10&recurring=true&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Phoenix&state=AZ&zip=85002&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=sinema&utm_content=3+-+Add+my+name&utm_campaign=KS_EM_FR_WL_180410_1_1&source=KS_EM_FR_WL_180410_1_1&refcode=KS_EM_FR_WL_180410_1_1")**

A Concerned Citizen

body:
Unfortunately, Leah’s primary opponent, Kevin Nicholson, is willing to insult hundreds of grassroots Republicans in Wisconsin. In a memo he released..

Friend,

Wisconsinites don’t need another politician who talks down to them.

Unfortunately, Leah’s primary opponent, Kevin Nicholson, is willing to insult hundreds of grassroots Republicans in Wisconsin. In a memo he released to the press, his team calls grassroots supporters like you "deep insiders," the “establishment” and of the "political class."

Conservatives would have never accomplished so many victories in our state if it weren’t for your support spending countless hours stapling yard signs, knocking on doors, and making phone calls at our victory centers.

Our activist team in Wisconsin is the furthest thing from the political elite. Alongside Gov. Walker, Leah fought to enact bold changes — from $8 billion in tax cuts to defunding Planned Parenthood to requiring a photo ID at the polls — all because it’s what you sent her to do at the state Capitol.

And unlike DC politicians, she delivered.

Kevin is wrong if he thinks treating dedicated volunteers poorly is the ticket to winning the Republican primary. Leah would never turn her back on the grassroots like that.

At least now we know what Kevin Nicholson really thinks of us. He just doesn't care about your "insider" votes as much as his outsider donors from DC.

Friend, he's just so out of touch with Wisconsin. That's why Leah is so determined to represent the Wisconsin grassroots. But we need your help:

That moment got me started on my mission to expand opportunities for education across Wisconsin, but we have a whole lot further to go. What are the [issues](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=e2985cc2c1b44be88836b486df8ef01e&_e=aE-oVyWIyeAMu0iZPXZWVYgkHVATTEbY7OtAdzt0cjgY0TpKM3JQqKDsb4mgC-X8mACZPbf2NcvRw2ZnfEORQObc6rcWBiRsjCtTspz2LPpVmvKjqBS5pRPcveBykAXCJZOAeGnCydq_pMVRJh4ihcRn796JLkmadH3hKPsnHxJXmgTdyRbxkHCdDCe036TEnnz2zSlPQloFFJA8MOJQvff0TOJNxjwPQIsk8bjNU_Cf_SyG-UitOWyMzsh5ookB7wn1rFXfk_Gyh_0yITGw28CSO-uRl8NXkeIeYfdR1jONJUnkdn5mnTVGAnBS62DunXiZncmcjXT4UExt005Bw2uZpbBZ0bySLI2PGSZSdkncUdJ97sZwGdYULlvPlSgm) that you care about? If the past few years have taught me anything, it’s that if you want to make change, you have to stand up and lead. So I’m asking you, to [stand up](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=e2985cc2c1b44be88836b486df8ef01e&_e=aE-oVyWIyeAMu0iZPXZWVYgkHVATTEbY7OtAdzt0cjhZFEqCHeABvC7-okaCOOkebvInaA9yBQDiAHldeoyJhIs3BVhGuLiCXv2OsSx-4J9Ci3L75L47_8_xRxvHYIL1-B0wcVZ_d1wrFhpVMAHjVY4-S8LjmILpo_Yw41YCqKqtsyPp9tziNZB4acTFj3E2ZgXk9piev6uF4ZzhMDMb3rwLKZX2E1xnYbhv3jY3Vkf-Tz2uQFvupDS-gj2mIpM8tAjsslo4HqwtVPXHfUhm-FLKidUe4H6peFIXb2GkZgsLq9aHCQrQ_g3YcXtwpg6MYo1r81hJJyQBSlVAiQyPow-yfd_u7CFpqv4dk0XunV2EDNIkHcVEkWubsyocujgp) to help me take the Wisconsin way to Washington. But we need your help: We’re so grateful for your support, and we hope you'll join us in supporting Leah who will always have the backs of hardworking Wisconsinites like you: Thanks, Team Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate

name: Tina Smith
subject: Women just caught up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
It's time to end the wage gap. | [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48404/c1f7015/69769e41/11431e16/2898304108/VEsH/ "https://facebook.com/TinaSmithMN/photos/a.2050320261858859.1073741828.2040270322863853/2545111805713033/?type=3&theater") Friend, As women, we had to work, on average, 99 days into 2018 to make the same amount as men did in 2017. That's right; it's April 10, and we've only just caught up. Women of color will need to work many more weeks to catch up. Today, Equal Pay Day reminds us that the fight for equal pay for equal work is far from over. [**Share this Equal Pay Day graphic on Facebook to show your support for closing the wage gap!**](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48404/c1f7015/69769e41/11431e16/2898304108/VEsE/ "https://facebook.com/TinaSmithMN/photos/a.2050320261858859.1073741828.2040270322863853/2545111805713033/?type=3&theater") [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48404/c1f7015/69769e41/11431e16/2898304108/VEsF/ "https://facebook.com/TinaSmithMN/photos/a.2050320261858859.1073741828.2040270322863853/2545111805713033/?type=3&theater") Whichever way you cut it, women are systematically paid less than men, at an average of 80 cents on the dollar. Over a 40 year career, that means an average woman and her family is being cheated out of $418,800. What would your family do with an extra half a million dollars? Whether it's financing a home, paying college tuition, or getting out of debt, I'm sure we can all think of plenty of ways to use those cheated wages. We must also acknowledge that it'll be a few more months until women of color reach their Equal Pay Day. The wage gap for black, Latina, and native women is even greater. That's just not right. **Here's what we can do.** The _Equal Pay Act_ passed over 50 years ago -- but it hasn't been enough to close the wage gap. States and cities across the country are taking action to address the pay gap, and they're making progress. **It's time to take these actions to a federal level.** We need to pass legislation to make sure employers can't retaliate when employees share their pay information among co-workers. We need to cut salary histories from negotiations, which can lock women into a cycle of lower pay. In fact, I co-sponsored the _Paycheck Fairness Act_ to address problems like these. I promise to be a fierce advocate for women's equality each and every day. But we need to keep the pressure on. [**Join me today in spreading the word about Equal Pay Day -- and let's end this wage gap.**](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48404/c1f7015/69769e41/11431e16/2898304108/VEsC/ "https://facebook.com/TinaSmithMN/photos/a.2050320261858859.1073741828.2040270322863853/2545111805713033/?type=3&theater") Thank you, Tina | [SPREAD THE WORD »](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48404/c1f7015/69769e41/11431e16/2898304108/VEsD/ "https://facebook.com/TinaSmithMN/photos/a.2050320261858859.1073741828.2040270322863853/2545111805713033/?type=3&theater")

name: The Sanders Institute
subject: Protect your vote in 2018: Why do we vote on Tuesdays?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Friend,
2018 is an election year. All four hundred and thirty-five Congressmen, a third of all Senators, thirty-six state and three territory governors, and over four hundred mayors will be elected this year.
America is notorious for its low voter turnout. Only a little over a third of eligible voters are predicted to vote in these 2018 "off-year" elections. So, the question becomes: Why is this happening? Why are so many voting-age Americans not voting?
Over the next six weeks, the Sanders Institute and founding fellow Senator Nina Turner will look at some of the glaring issues in our voting system.
**[This week, we will begin by looking at why Americans vote on Tuesday.](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ac31/bed43eb/57a15e3e/75fe7c9d/350768828/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/tuesday-and-early-voting")**
[](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ac31/bed43eb/57a15e3e/75fe7c9d/350768828/VEsE/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/tuesday-and-early-voting")
American voters are shackled by laws created two centuries ago. It is time our voting laws reflected the modern world.
Thanks for staying engaged,
The Sanders Institute Team

It's 2018 -- there's no reason that a woman should make 21 cents less per hour than a man doing the same work.

But it's the reality today. And even worse, Pennsylvania Republicans keep fighting to roll back women's rights by refusing to close the pay gap.

[**Today, on Equal Pay Day, it's time to stand up for equal pay for equal work. Will you join me and Governor Wolf in demanding the Pennsylvania General Assembly close the pay gap?**](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecc3b/73a2bc12/3cd5381a/1fcd076b/4090396829/VEsE/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/equal-pay-day-petition?utm_campaign=em18_180410_eqpay_adv_gq_ful__td&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180410_eqpay_adv_gq_ful__td&utm_content=2+-+Today+on+Equal+Pay+Day+its+time+to+stand")

Equal Pay Day marks the end of the extra days the average woman must work to finally earn the same amount as men earned in the previous year nationally.

But the pay gap in Pennsylvania is even larger than the national average. So women here must work even longer -- and it's time for that to change.

Governor Wolf was an advocate for pay equity long before he even took office. He's continued to fight for protections against pay discrimination and he's fought to combat sexual harassment and other challenges that prevent women from earning the living they deserve.

But some Harrisburg politicians have ignored the pay gap, pretended it wasn't real, or actively fought to make it worse.

In order to protect the progress we've made -- and to finally close the pay gap -- we need to show that we're standing with Governor Wolf in demanding action.

[**Add your name now, and tell the Pennsylvania General Assembly: "It's time for equal pay for equal work!"**](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecc3b/73a2bc12/3cd5381a/1fcd076a/4090396829/VEsF/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/equal-pay-day-petition?utm_campaign=em18_180410_eqpay_adv_gq_ful__td&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180410_eqpay_adv_gq_ful__td&utm_content=3+-+Add+your+name+now+and+tell+the+Pennsylva")

Thanks for taking action,

Tori DeGiosio
Digital Director
Tom Wolf for Governor

name: Christina Hale
subject: fairness and hard work
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
April 10 is Equal Pay Day -- the day when the average American woman finally finishes earning what the average man earned in the previous year. Can you believe it? We have to work over three months mo |
---

| | | [](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c205d/7377f838/3e0c9a33/51fa832a/276885866/VEsH/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/join-women-for-joe")
---

Peter,

**April 10 is Equal Pay Day -- the day when the average American woman finally finishes earning what the average man earned in the previous year.** Can you believe it? We have to work over three months more to bring home as much pay as our male counterparts earn in one year. Black and Latina women make even less. We must set things right. The clock is literally ticking.

We must do something now for ourselves, but more importantly, for the young women entering the workforce behind us. We sent astronauts to the moon in the 1960s. This is 2018, and women and men should be paid the same for doing the same work. Period.

You probably learned how to dance the daycare shuffle like I did. Struggling to pick your kids up on time is hard enough, and paying for quality care is a tremendous challenge for far too many. Raising my son as a single mom had plenty of obstacles -- including the fact that my male colleagues took home more pay than I did. That is why I decided to stand up for other women like me and advocate for issues that really matter to people, like equal pay.

Right now we have a commonsense Hoosier senator who has committed to stand up for equal pay for women in our country. Joe Donnelly has already voted for the Lilly Ledbetter Act in the House and the Paycheck Fairness Act in the Senate. I am proud to support a leader who is looking out for us -- if you are too, **[will you join our campaign's Women for Joe group? >>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c205d/7377f838/3e0c9a33/51fa832a/276885866/VEsE/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/join-women-for-joe")**
[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c205d/7377f838/3e0c9a33/51fa832a/276885866/VEsF/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/join-women-for-joe")
---
**Fair pay isn 't a partisan issue, it's a family issue. And it hits hard.**

Hoosier women who are employed full-time miss out on nearly _$16 billion_ every year due to the wage gap. If that gap were eliminated, those wages could help the average woman in Indiana afford:
* over a year of rent
* nearly 2 years of child care
* or the full cost of tuition at a community college.

Wage equality will make a significant, practical difference for people. I don't know about you, but I am still paying off my student loans. A working woman's life is never easy, and I am extremely grateful to all of the women who helped me along the way, and believe me, that is one long list. Joe has always had our back. That is a very big deal, and that is why I am committed to helping him.

At a time when **nearly 100,000 Indiana households headed by women fall below the poverty line** , addressing the wage gap must be a top priority for lawmakers. And for Joe, it is.

You can help us in this effort to ensure that Joe returns to Washington. Clearly we all need to buckle up, as this is going to be an incredibly tough election cycle. Washington special interests are relentless in their attempts to silence Joe's independent voice -- they have fought long and hard to hold women back, and your support now will make an important difference. **[Will you please add your name to join our Women for Donnelly group and show Joe that you support his efforts?>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c205d/7377f838/3e0c9a33/51fa832a/276885866/VEsC/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/join-women-for-joe")**

Thank you, Peter. Together, let's send Joe back to Washington to work on the issues that matter most to us.

Christina Hale

****[**CONTRIBUTE**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c205d/7377f838/3e0c9a33/51fa8325/276885866/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180410.2&amount=25")****
---

|
---

name: Susie Turnbull
subject: A seat at the table.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friend,
Today is Equal Pay Day, a day that symbolizes how far into the year women must work to earn what men earned in the previous year. It is a wake-up call every single year.
According to a study by the National Partnership for Women & Families, Maryland women are typically paid 84 cents for every dollar paid to men. That shakes out to an average of $10,074 less than a male doing**the same job**.
I've been in the workforce since I started at a minimum wage job at age 16. Now, decades later after founding, leading and nurturing women's organizations and seeing the gap firsthand, I've done a lot of thinking about where the movement for women's rights and equality stands.
It isn't a pretty picture. As is often the case with our nation, we sometimes take two steps backward before moving forward. The Trump Presidency is many more steps backward, but the fire that has been ignited among women in our nation, as a result, will be the march forward our nation needs.
**[Will you contribute $5 right now to say you are ready to join the resistance and continue our fight for equality and a better standard of living for women across our state?](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7a4/7ab0e2c4/7fae456a/349a666d/654621248/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDQxMDhEIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st4-18?refcode=OHQ04108D")**
When women are paid less, families suffer. And our voices have been missing here In Maryland where for the first time in decades women do not hold any seats in our congressional delegation nor any elected statewide offices. With healthcare premiums rising, our schools chronically underfunded and falling in national rankings, and women facing persistent wage discrimination, **we need women who have been in the trenches on these issues to have a seat at the table again, to set the agenda and to build the coalitions needed for change**.
Women are the backbones of families across this nation, and more mothers in the U.S. are breadwinners than ever before. But the wage gap women experience is even starker for women of color, where Black women are paid 69 cents, Latinas are paid 47 cents, and Asian women are paid 83 cents for every dollar paid to white, non-Hispanic men1.
I've never run for public office before, but in many roles over many years, I have helped pave the way for women across our state and nation to run and to win. This year is different. The inspiration I felt following the Women's March has pushed me to offer my 40 years of policy experience and as a political organizer and activist to help get Maryland back to doing big things again.
**[Our campaign will fight for issues that I care about as a woman, a mother, a grandmother, and a citizen of this state. If you stand with me, chip in $5 to us today!](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7a4/7ab0e2c4/7fae456a/349a666d/654621248/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDQxMDhEIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st4-18?refcode=OHQ04108D")**
This Equal Pay Day, our fight for equality goes on, but with a vigor never before seen. The future is female, and together we are ensuring that women will write the final chapter of the Trump-era. Women will lead our state and nation to better days, and I remained hopeful and inspired to make our future brighter than ever before.
Thanks,
Susie

**[CHIP IN](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7a4/7ab0e2c4/7fae456a/349a666d/654621248/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDQxMDhEIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st4-18?refcode=OHQ04108D")**

1[http://www.nationalpartnership.org/research-library/workplace-fairness/fair-pay/4-2017-md-wage-gap.pdf ](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7a4/7ab0e2c4/7fae456a/349a666c/654621248/VEsC/ "http://www.nationalpartnership.org/research-library/workplace-fairness/fair-pay/4-2017-md-wage-gap.pdf")

* * *
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER

Email is one of the most critical tools for our campaign, but you can let us know if you would like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7a4/7ab0e2c4/7fae456a/349a666e/654621248/VEsA/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=emfooter") going forward. Hello,

Today, women in North Carolina earn just 82 cents for every dollar men earn.

That's just wrong, especially considering the critical role women play in our economy.

Unfortunately, for women of color, the pay gap is even larger.

**I want a North Carolina that works for everybody -- and that includes equal pay for equal work.[Add your name today support equal pay for equal work.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203475/2fde7c66/4626916f/1569d745/3980374187/VEsE/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/add-your-name-for-equal-pay?utm_campaign=em18_180410_eqpay_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em18_180410_eqpay_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+today+support+equal+pay+fo")**

As I travel around the state, I am constantly meeting with hardworking women who are essential to our state and our communities.

The fact that women are paid less than men for the same work is unacceptable.

[**Add your name today to support equal pay for equal work.**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203475/2fde7c66/4626916f/1569d744/3980374187/VEsF/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/add-your-name-for-equal-pay?utm_campaign=em18_180410_eqpay_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em18_180410_eqpay_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_content=3+-+Add+your+name+today+to+support+equal+pay")

Thanks for all you continue to do for our state,

Roy

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: $0.82 for every dollar
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Gina Raimondo
body:
Friend --
Today is 'Equal Pay Day.' The date signifies how far into the year the average woman in the U.S. has to work to make what men made in the previous calendar year.
This request could not be more urgent. Today is a powerful reminder: it's time to close the wage gap.
**[Donate $10 to our campaign to help close the pay gap and ensure equal pay for equal work.](https://organizing.ginaraimondo.com/page/m/1f41a0bc/5e076a9e/5471cc1f/14427685/330401875/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/for-equal-pay?refcode=180410")**
Here in Rhode Island, women make 82 cents for every dollar men make. For women of color, the statistics are even more staggering: black women in the state make 58 cents and Latina women only make 51 cents for every dollar their male counterparts take home.
Will you join our effort to combat wage discrimination in Rhode Island? **[Click here to donate now!](https://organizing.ginaraimondo.com/page/m/1f41a0bc/5e076a9e/5471cc1f/14427685/330401875/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/for-equal-pay?refcode=180410")**
Thank you. Let's keep going.
Gina

Per Rhode Island State Law, individuals may contribute a maximum of $1,000 per year. Business and corporate checks are prohibited.

name: [Education Survey] Team Kaine
subject: Need input on education in Virginia >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Take a minute to tell Team Kaine your opinions about our education system. -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|
---
[ ](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1352/3e0906/62116230/3e34bb39/2742021341/VEsH/ "https://www.timkaine.com/")

| | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Hey Derek,

Teachers across the country are standing up for their students and speaking out about the importance of expanding resources for schools so that we can provide our nation's children with an education they deserve.

Tim and his wife Anne, who served as the Virginia Secretary of Education, have long been fierce advocates for reforming education throughout their careers - and they are keenly aware that providing a quality public education is one of the most important things we can do for our kids.

**Our teachers deserve fair pay, our students deserve the books and supplies they need to learn and grow, and schools deserve the resources they need to create manageable class sizes so that all children can be heard and mentored.**

We need to be equipping our teachers with resources to educate our children - not with guns and ammo in a forced attempt to fill the dangerous void created by the inaction of legislators.

**But we want to hear from you: Will you take just a minute to fill out our brief survey on education?[Click here to start the survey >>](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1352/3e0906/62116230/3e34bb38/2742021341/VEsE/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/education-survey?source=20180410_1_SURV_TEAM_Education")**

Thanks for weighing in! Your input is so important to us.

_Team Kaine_
